preview_text:
The model favors Morocco at 61%, with Scotland's win probability at just 13%. The underdog's chance reflects a substantial gap in the forecast, though Morocco's advantage is not overwhelming given the 26% draw probability baked into the model's base case.

The expected spread shows Morocco as clear favorite but with a meaningful draw rate that narrows the effective two-way gap. Scotland's 13% win chance sits well below its 26% draw chance, suggesting the model sees the teams as mismatched in quality yet susceptible to a stalemate outcome. A competitive second half is plausible even if the baseline tilts decisively toward a Moroccan result.

The model and market diverge meaningfully. Sportsbooks assign Scotland 23%, a 10-percentage-point lift above the model's 13%, and price Morocco at 48%, roughly 13 points lower. Polymarket data show uniform 33% pricing across all three outcomes, suggesting minimal consensus on either side at that venue. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the gaps warrant attention from bettors seeking value.
